summary: The case shows daytime meal omission with nocturnal hunger and impulsive night eating, along with perceived misalignment of hunger cues. Proposed modifiable levers span biological (nutrition, interoception, sleep), psychological (urge-regulation, psychoeducation), and social (accountability) domains, including interactions (e.g., sleep improvements aiding appetite signals, interoception training informing food choices). All predictors are actionable and adjustable, with clear criteria targets and simple measurements.

solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a fixed daytime meal schedule (e.g., breakfast, lunch, 2 planned snacks) with calendar reminders and a simple 5-minute meal prep routine to ensure daytime caloric intake remains consistent.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Externalized meal structure reduces daytime forgetfulness, stabilizes energy availability, and decreases the risk of nocturnal hunger driven by extended daytime fasting.
solutions.measurement: Proportion of days with 3 meals and 2 snacks logged by 7pm; number of daytime meals skipped per week.

solutions.solution_candidate: Enable smartphone reminders and a simple meal-logging app that prompts at morning and midday, with quick meal options to choose from.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Digital prompts reduce cognitive load and forgetfulness; recording meals increases awareness and adherence.
solutions.measurement: App engagement rate; days with daytime meals logged; total meals logged per week.

solutions.solution_candidate: Weekly guided interoceptive exercises (e.g., hunger-satiety ratings before meals; mindful tasting) to recalibrate hunger cues and alignment between signals and intake.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improves accuracy of internal hunger cues, reducing the perception of signals as 'broken' and guiding more appropriate eating behavior.
solutions.measurement: Cue-rating alignment score (pre/post); self-reported hunger-signal accuracy; mismatch episodes per week.

solutions.solution_candidate: Plan a fixed, protein-rich evening snack and define a last-meal window (e.g., last intake by 7-8pm) to reduce nocturnal hunger and poor late-night choices.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Satiety stabilization in the evening lowers the urge to binge and improves the quality of late-night choices.
solutions.measurement: Number of nocturnal eating episodes per week; quality of late-night choices (nutrient-dense vs. energy-dense).

solutions.solution_candidate: Teach and practice urge surfing and a 10-minute delay strategy during nighttime hunger; apply brief distress tolerance and alternative activities.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Delaying immediate eating weakens automatic reinforcement of snacking and builds self-regulation during hunger peaks.
solutions.measurement: Frequency of late-night urges leading to eating; percentage of urges delayed >10 minutes; self-reported urge intensity.

solutions.solution_candidate: Standardize bedtimes/wake times, optimize morning light exposure, and cue caffeine timing to improve sleep quality and daytime energy.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Better sleep regularity stabilizes appetite hormones and reduces nocturnal hunger driven by sleep disruption.
solutions.measurement: Sleep consistency score (bedtime/wake time variance); average sleep duration; daytime energy reports.

solutions.solution_candidate: Assign an accountability partner or group for checking in on meals and food choices; incorporate family/household support and meal-sharing routines.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social support enhances adherence to daytime eating plans and improves food-choice quality through norms and accountability.
solutions.measurement: Days with partner check-ins; perceived support quality; shared meals per week.

solutions.solution_candidate: Deliver a concise psychoeducation module covering hunger cues, energy balance, and why hunger signals can misfire; include practical, actionable tips and self-monitoring strategies.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Increases cognitive understanding and self-efficacy to manage hunger signals, meal timing, and nocturnal eating habits.
solutions.measurement: Knowledge gain score; self-efficacy rating for hunger management; application of tips (self-report).
